1295.iwh_en: It was narrated from Adi ibn Hatim (may Allah be pleased with him) that he asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about hunting with a dog. He said: 'What your dog catches for you, eat it, for its catching is its slaughter. But if you find another dog with it and you fear that it had caught it together with your dog and killed it, do not eat it, for you mentioned the name of Allah over your dog but not over the other one.' He also asked about hunting with a mi'rad (a stick) and mentioned the same ruling.
1295.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Adi ibn Hatim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia bertanya kepada Rasulullah ﷺ tentang berburu dengan anjing. Beliau bersabda: 'Apa yang ditangkap oleh anjingmu untukmu, makanlah, karena tangkapannya adalah sembelihan baginya. Tetapi jika kamu menemukan anjing lain bersamanya dan kamu khawatir bahwa anjing itu telah menangkapnya bersama anjingmu dan membunuhnya, jangan makan, karena kamu hanya menyebut nama Allah atas anjingmu dan tidak atas yang lain.' Dia juga bertanya tentang berburu dengan mi'rad (tongkat) dan menyebutkan hukum yang sama.

1296.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever keeps a dog other than a hunting dog or a livestock-guarding dog, two qirats are deducted from his deeds every day.'
1296.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang memelihara anjing selain anjing pemburu atau anjing penjaga ternak, maka setiap hari dua qirat dikurangi dari amalannya.'

1297.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sufyan ibn Abi Zuhayr (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Whoever keeps a dog that is not used for farming or herding, one qirat is deducted from his deeds every day.' They said: Did you yourself hear this from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ? He said: 'Yes, by the Lord of this mosque.'
1297.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sufyan ibn Abi Zuhayr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang memelihara anjing yang tidak berguna untuk pertanian maupun untuk menjaga ternak, setiap hari satu qirat dikurangi dari amalannya.' Mereka berkata: Apakah kamu sendiri mendengar ini dari Rasulullah ﷺ? Ia berkata: 'Ya, demi Rabb masjid ini.'

1298.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ commanded the killing of dogs, then said: 'What do I have to do with dogs?' Then he permitted the dog used for herding and the hunting dog.
1298.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan untuk membunuh anjing-anjing, kemudian bersabda: 'Apa urusanku dengan anjing-anjing?' Kemudian beliau mengizinkan anjing untuk menggembalakan ternak dan anjing berburu.

1299.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded the killing of dogs.'
1299.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkan untuk membunuh anjing-anjing.'

1300.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Were it not that dogs are a nation among nations, I would command that they all be killed. But kill of them every pure black one.' Said ibn Amir said: 'Al-bahim means totally black.'
1300.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seandainya anjing-anjing bukan merupakan suatu umat di antara umat-umat, niscaya aku perintahkan untuk membunuh semuanya. Namun bunuhlah dari mereka setiap yang berwarna hitam pekat.' Said ibn Amir berkata: 'Al-bahim artinya yang seluruhnya hitam.'

1301.iwh_en: It was narrated from Adi ibn Hatim (may Allah be pleased with him) that he asked the Prophet ﷺ about hunting with a mi'rad (a flat-headed arrow). He said: 'If it strikes with its sharp edge, eat it. But if it strikes with its flat side and kills it, then it is waqidh (killed by blunt force), so do not eat it.'
1301.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Adi ibn Hatim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ tentang berburu dengan mi'rad (anak panah berkepala datar). Beliau bersabda: 'Jika ia mengenai dengan ujung tajamnya, makanlah. Tetapi jika ia mengenai dengan sisi datarnya dan membunuhnya, maka itu adalah waqidh (yang mati karena pukulan benda tumpul), maka jangan makan.'

1302.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Abi Awfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We went on seven military campaigns with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ eating locusts.'
1302.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Abi Awfa (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami berperang dalam tujuh peperangan bersama Rasulullah ﷺ sambil memakan belalang.'

1303.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man asked the Prophet ﷺ and said: We sail the sea and carry little water with us; if we use it for ablution we become thirsty — may we perform ablution with seawater? The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'It is purifying, its water, and lawful, its dead.'
1303.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: Kami berlayar di lautan dan hanya membawa sedikit air bersama kami; jika kami gunakan untuk berwudhu maka kami akan kehausan — bolehkah kami berwudhu dengan air laut? Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Laut itu suci airnya, dan halal bangkainya.'

1304.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent us out with three hundred men. We were struck by hunger until we came to the sea and it had cast up a creature. We ate from it until our bodies recovered. Abu Ubayda took one of its ribs and set it up, then loaded the tallest man in the army on the largest camel in the army and they passed under it — this is the meaning of it.'
1304.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ mengutus kami dengan tiga ratus orang. Kami ditimpa kelaparan hingga kami tiba di laut dan laut telah memuntahkan seekor makhluk. Kami memakannya hingga tubuh kami pulih. Abu Ubayda mengambil salah satu tulang rusuknya dan mendirikannya, kemudian menaikkan orang paling tinggi di pasukan di atas unta terbesar di pasukan dan mereka melewati di bawahnya — inilah maknanya.'

1305.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: We startled a hare while we were at Marr al-Zahran. The people ran after it and became tired, so I caught it and brought it to Abu Talha. He slaughtered it and sent its haunches — or its thighs (Shu'bah was uncertain) — to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who accepted them.
1305.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: Kami mengejutkan seekor kelinci saat kami berada di Marr al-Zahran. Orang-orang berlari mengejarnya dan kelelahan, maka aku menangkapnya dan membawanya kepada Abu Talhah. Ia menyembelihnya dan mengirimkan bokong atau pahanya — Syu'bah ragu — kepada Rasulullah ﷺ, dan beliau menerimanya.

1306.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muhammad ibn Safwan (may Allah be pleased with him) that he passed by the Prophet ﷺ carrying two rabbits hanging, and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I entered my family's flock and hunted these two rabbits, but I could not find an iron blade to slaughter them, so I slaughtered them with a flint stone. May I eat them?' He said: 'Yes.'
1306.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Muhammad ibn Safwan (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia melewati Nabi ﷺ sambil membawa dua ekor kelinci yang digantung, dan berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, aku masuk ke kawanan ternak keluargaku dan berburu dua ekor kelinci ini, tetapi aku tidak menemukan pisau besi untuk menyembelihnya, maka aku menyembelihnya dengan batu api. Bolehkah aku memakannya?' Beliau bersabda: 'Ya.'

1307.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ was asked about the dhabb (desert monitor lizard). He said: 'I do not eat it and I do not declare it forbidden.'
1307.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ ditanya tentang dhabb (biawak gurun). Beliau bersabda: 'Aku tidak memakannya dan aku tidak mengharamkannya.'

1308.iwh_en: It was narrated from Thabit ibn Wadiah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a dhabb (desert monitor lizard) was brought to the Prophet ﷺ and he said: 'A nation that was transformed — and Allah knows best.'
1308.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Thabit ibn Wadiah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seekor dhabb (biawak gurun) dibawa kepada Nabi ﷺ, maka beliau bersabda: 'Suatu umat yang telah diubah wujudnya — dan Allah lebih mengetahui.'

1309.iwh_en: It was narrated from Khalid ibn al-Walid (may Allah be pleased with him), known as the Sword of Allah, that he entered with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ upon Maymunah, wife of the Prophet ﷺ, who was his aunt and the aunt of Ibn Abbas. He found with her a roasted dhabb that her sister Hufaydah bint al-Harith had brought from Najd. She presented the dhabb to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he rarely extended his hand to food before being told what it was and it being named for him. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ stretched out his hand toward the dhabb, but a woman from among those present said: 'Inform the Messenger of Allah ﷺ what you have presented to him.' They said: 'This is a dhabb.' So the Messenger of Allah ﷺ withdrew his hand. Khalid ibn al-Walid said: 'Do you forbid the dhabb, O Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'No, but it was not in the land of my people, so I find myself averse to it.' Khalid said: I then pulled it toward me and ate it, while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ watched, and he did not forbid me.
1309.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Khalid ibn al-Walid (radhiyallahu 'anhu), yang dikenal sebagai Pedang Allah, bahwa ia masuk bersama Rasulullah ﷺ menemui Maimunah, istri Nabi ﷺ, yang merupakan bibinya dan bibi Ibn Abbas. Ia mendapati di sisinya seekor dhabb yang dipanggang yang dibawa oleh saudarinya Hufaidah binti al-Harits dari Najd. Ia menyajikan dhabb tersebut kepada Rasulullah ﷺ, dan beliau jarang mengulurkan tangannya kepada makanan sebelum diberitahu apa itu dan disebutkan namanya kepadanya. Rasulullah ﷺ mengulurkan tangannya ke arah dhabb, tetapi seorang wanita dari antara yang hadir berkata: 'Beritahukan kepada Rasulullah ﷺ apa yang kalian sajikan kepadanya.' Mereka berkata: 'Ini adalah dhabb.' Maka Rasulullah ﷺ menarik kembali tangannya. Khalid ibn al-Walid berkata: 'Apakah Anda mengharamkan dhabb, wahai Rasulullah?' Beliau bersabda: 'Tidak, tetapi dhabb tidak ada di negeri kaumku, maka aku mendapati diri enggan terhadapnya.' Khalid berkata: Aku pun menariknya dan memakannya, sementara Rasulullah ﷺ menyaksikan, dan beliau tidak melarangku.

1310.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Waqid al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to Madinah, the people were cutting off the humps of camels and the fat tails of sheep. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whatever is cut from a living animal is dead meat (maytah).'
1310.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Waqid al-Laythi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ tiba di Madinah, orang-orang memotong punuk-punuk unta dan ekor-ekor berlemak kambing. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apa yang dipotong dari hewan yang masih hidup adalah bangkai.'
